title: Agropyron duplicatum
taxonomy: Agropyron duplicatum, also known as Elymus duplicatus, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Elymus, which is a group of grasses commonly known as wild ryegrasses or spike grasses. The genus Elymus is part of the subfamily Pooideae, which is a large and diverse group of grasses. The subfamily Pooideae is part of the family Poaceae, which is the largest and most widely distributed family of flowering plants. Elymus duplicatus is native to the mountainous regions of western North America.
